2605.18648.title: An Assessment of Human vs. Model Uncertainty in Soft-Label Learning and Calibration
2605.18648.paper_summary: Central to human-aligned AI is understanding the benefits of human-elicited labels over synthetic alternatives. While human soft-labels improve calibration by capturing uncertainty, prior studies conflate these benefits with the implicit correction of mislabeled data (mode shifts), obscuring true effects of soft-labels. We present a controlled audit of soft-label learning across MNIST and a synthetic variant, re-annotating subsets to extract human uncertainty. By decoupling soft-label supervision from underlying label mode shifts, we show that while human soft-labels do provide accuracy gains, their larger value lies in acting as a regularizer that improves model calibration on difficult samples and promotes stable convergence across training runs. Dataset cartography reveals models trained on human soft-labels mirror human uncertainty, whereas those trained on synthetic labels fail to align with humans. Broadly, this work provides a diagnostic testbed for human-AI uncertainty alignment.
2605.18648.paper_summary_zh: 人類對齊的人工智慧的核心在於理解人類引導的標籤相較於合成替代品的優勢。雖然人類的軟標籤透過捕捉不確定性來改善校準，但先前的研究將這些好處與錯誤標記數據的隱性修正（模式轉變）混為一談，模糊了軟標籤的真實效果。我們針對 MNIST 和一個合成變體進行了軟標籤學習的控制審計，重新標註子集以提取人類的不確定性。通過將軟標籤監督與基礎標籤模式轉變解耦，我們顯示雖然人類的軟標籤確實提供了準確性提升，但它們更大的價值在於作為一種正則化器，改善在困難樣本上的模型校準並促進訓練過程中的穩定收斂。數據集地圖學顯示，基於人類軟標籤訓練的模型反映了人類的不確定性，而基於合成標籤訓練的模型則未能與人類對齊。總體而言，這項工作提供了一個人類與人工智慧不確定性對齊的診斷測試平台。
2605.18648.author: Maja Pavlovic et.al.
2605.18648.authors: Maja Pavlovic, Silviu Paun, Massimo Poesio
